title: Module Augmentation

statement.markdown:
## Module Augmentation
Imagine you are working on a large TypeScript project and you want to extend the functionality of a third-party module without modifying its original code. You can achieve this by using module augmentation, which allows you to add new properties or interfaces to an existing module.

constraints: ["You can only use the `declare global` syntax to augment the module.","You cannot modify the original module's code.","You can add new properties or interfaces to the existing module."]
